features.summary: Disney reported Q2 2021 adjusted EPS of $0.79, up 32% year-over-year, showing recovery across business segments. Disney+ reached nearly 104 million paid subscribers, remaining on track for 230-260 million by fiscal 2024. Parks showed strong recovery with Walt Disney World and Shanghai operating at reduced capacity but achieving positive contribution margins, while Disneyland reopened in late April to strong demand. Direct-to-consumer operating results improved over $500 million versus prior year, driven by Hulu and ESPN+ growth.
features.management_outlook: Management expressed optimism about continued recovery across all business segments, particularly parks operations where domestic intent to visit has returned to 2019 levels. They expect to benefit from CDC guidance allowing vaccinated guests to go maskless and plan to increase park capacities accordingly. For streaming, they remain confident in reaching subscriber guidance despite expecting slower net additions in the second half due to IPL suspension in India and delayed Star Plus launch in Latin America. Content production is ramping up significantly with increased output planned across all platforms.
features.risks: Key risks include ongoing COVID-19 impacts, particularly the suspension of IPL cricket matches in India affecting Disney+ Hotstar subscribers and advertising revenue. International park operations remain challenged with Disneyland Paris still closed and Hong Kong operating limited days. Linear networks face significant headwinds with ESPN programming costs expected to increase $1.2 billion in Q3 due to return of major sporting events. Theatrical exhibition recovery remains uncertain, requiring flexible distribution strategies.
features.key_drivers: Primary growth drivers include strong parks recovery with double-digit per capita spending growth and improved operational efficiency through technology and yield management. Streaming momentum continues with successful content slate including Marvel series driving engagement and subscriber growth. Sports rights portfolio expansion with new MLB and La Liga deals strengthens ESPN+ positioning. International expansion of Disney+ and Star brand, along with planned price increases, should drive ARPU improvements across the streaming portfolio.
